In several parts of Belfast, police and firefighters struggled to contain mobs of anti-immigrant militants into the early hours of Wednesday as they targeted ethnic minority households, restaurants and shops. Police said dozens of homes were torched, along with many of the immigrants’ cars and at least one city bus, as authorities focused on getting threatened people to safety, not on confronting and arresting attackers.
